name: SIROS ID
description: A personal identity document issued by the SIROS Foundation, verified via FaceTec biometric liveness + photo-ID match.
$comment: Lightweight local test fixture mirroring the claims schema in demo-credentials. vct intentionally points at a real, live https:// URL (this fork branch on GitHub) rather than a urn: identifier or the stale production registry.siros.org URL. Reason: vc-apigw does NOT embed the VCTM into the issued credential JWT header (confirmed by reading vc/pkg/sdjwtvc/methods.go BuildCredentialWithSigner -- the header only ever contains typ/kid/alg) -- it only uses the local vctm_file_path to derive the vct claim and validate claims. So wallet-common getSdJwtVcMetadata has exactly one way to resolve type metadata: a live HTTP(S) fetch of the vct URL. A urn: vct (as previously tried, matching vctm_pid_arf_1_8.json) resolves to nothing at all in this environment -- no HTTP fetch (not a URL), no vctResolutionEngine hit (unknown custom urn), no embedded-header fallback (never written) -- so ALL type metadata (claim display labels AND rendering.svg_templates) was silently empty, not just the SVG rendering. Pointing vct at this fork branch (serving our corrected vctm/siros_id.vctm.json, with proper claims[].svg_id and a working https-hosted svg_templates entry) lets the live-fetch path actually succeed with correct data. Once the demo-credentials PR merges and registry.siros.org republishes, this should be switched back to the real production URL.

display.name: SIROS ID
display.description: Personal identity document issued by the SIROS Foundation.

display.label: First name
display.description: Current first name(s), including middle name(s), of the holder.

display.label: Last name
display.description: Current last name(s) or surname(s) of the holder.

display.label: Date of birth
display.description: Full birth date (day, month, year) of the holder.

display.label: Sex
display.description: ISO/IEC 5218 code (0 = not known, 1 = male, 2 = female, 9 = not applicable).
